name: ExtensionAuditEvent_Outcome_Code
title: R5: Whether the event succeeded or failed (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `AuditEvent.outcome.code` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`AuditEvent.outcome.code` 1..1 `Coding`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `AuditEvent.outcome.code` 1..1 `Coding`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `AuditEvent.outcome.code` has a context of AuditEvent.outcome based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AuditEvent`.
Element `AuditEvent.outcome.code` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Whether the event succeeded or failed (new)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.outcome.code` has a context of AuditEvent.outcome based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AuditEvent`.
Element `AuditEvent.outcome.code` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
In some cases a "success" may be partial, for example, an incomplete or interrupted transfer of a radiological study. For the purpose of establishing accountability, these distinctions are not relevant.

element.short: Whether the event succeeded or failed
element.definition: Indicates whether the event succeeded or failed.
element.comment: In some cases a "success" may be partial, for example, an incomplete or interrupted transfer of a radiological study. For the purpose of establishing accountability, these distinctions are not relevant.
